issue.author: vogelsgesang

comments.author: vogelsgesang
comments.body: godbolt repro: https://godbolt.org/z/enrGzMWGW (thanks @philnik777 for creating this!)

comments.author: vogelsgesang
comments.body:
Compiling with clang15 and libc++14, the error disappears: https://godbolt.org/z/Mz3d4h35o.
At the same time, clang14 with libc++15 also doesn't show the bug: https://godbolt.org/z/r7EqKc6Ko.
If I interpret this correctly, this means that both libc++ and clang/asan changed something between LLVM-14 and LLVM-15 such that the bug only occurs when combining clang-15 and libc++15

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: I used `build/bin/clang++ -std=c++2b -O2 -fsanitize=address -nostdinc -fno-exceptions` with this preprocessed source: https://gist.github.com/nikic/3853b2f2ef0b8b967a64279973239a3b This uses the exit code to indicate failure, to avoid the need to link against an actual libc++.

comments.author: tru
comments.body:
This one seems like it would be great to fix in 15.x

15.0.1 is out tomorrow, so I wonder if it makes sense to revert the problematic commit for this release and then fix it properly later - or if you think you can find a solution before tomorrow @fhahn ?

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body: I've pushed 582f8ef19fe791e66c0bbe0 to effectively revert the original commit. I'll put it up for cherry-picking in a bit.

comments.author: tru
comments.body: Thanks for the quick fix @fhahn ! Will merge this before I do 15.0.1 tomorrow!

issue.title: libc++15: Incorrect results from `vector::insert` in ASAN build

tests.test_body:
target triple = "arm64-apple-macosx"

define void @test_widen_ptr_induction(ptr %ptr.start.1) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %ptr.iv = phi ptr [ %ptr.start.1, %entry ], [ %ptr.iv.next, %loop ]
  %cmp.i.i.i.i = icmp ne ptr %ptr.iv, null
  tail call void @llvm.assume(i1 %cmp.i.i.i.i)
  store i8 0, ptr %ptr.iv, align 1
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %ptr.iv.next = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %ptr.iv, i64 1
  %ec = icmp eq i64 %iv, 10000
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(inaccessiblemem: write)
declare void @llvm.assume(i1 noundef) #0

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(inaccessiblemem: write) }

define ptr @test(ptr %start.1, ptr %start.2, ptr %end) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv.1 = phi ptr [ %start.1, %entry ], [ %iv.1.next, %loop ]
  %iv.2 = phi ptr [ %start.2, %entry ], [ %iv.2.next, %loop ]
  store i64 0, ptr %iv.2, align 4
  %iv.2.next = getelementptr inbounds ptr, ptr %iv.2, i64 1
  %iv.1.next = getelementptr inbounds ptr, ptr %iv.1, i64 1
  %cmp.i.i.not.i = icmp eq ptr %iv.2.next, %end
  br i1 %cmp.i.i.not.i, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  %res.lcssa = phi ptr [ %iv.1, %loop ]
  ret ptr %res.lcssa
}

target triple = "aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define void @pointer_induction_used_as_vector(ptr noalias %start.1, ptr noalias %start.2, i64 %N) {
entry:
  br label %loop.body

loop.body:                                        ; preds = %loop.body, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop.body ]
  %ptr.iv.1 = phi ptr [ %start.1, %entry ], [ %ptr.iv.1.next, %loop.body ]
  %ptr.iv.2 = phi ptr [ %start.2, %entry ], [ %ptr.iv.2.next, %loop.body ]
  %ptr.iv.1.next = getelementptr inbounds ptr, ptr %ptr.iv.1, i64 1
  %ptr.iv.2.next = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %ptr.iv.2, i64 1
  store ptr %ptr.iv.2.next, ptr %ptr.iv.1, align 8
  %lv = load i8, ptr %ptr.iv.2, align 1
  %add = add i8 %lv, 1
  store i8 %add, ptr %ptr.iv.2, align 1
  %iv.next = add nuw i64 %iv, 1
  %c = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, %N
  br i1 %c, label %loop.body, label %exit, !llvm.loop !0

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.body
  ret void
}

!0 = distinct !{!0, !1, !2, !3}
!1 = !{!"llvm.loop.interleave.count", i32 1}
!2 = !{!"llvm.loop.vectorize.width", i32 2}
!3 = !{!"llvm.loop.vectorize.scalable.enable", i1 true}

target triple = "aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define void @pointer_induction(ptr noalias %start, i64 %N) {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %ptr.phi = phi ptr [ %ptr.phi.next, %for.body ], [ %start, %entry ]
  %index = phi i64 [ %index_nxt, %for.body ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %index_nxt = add i64 %index, 1
  %0 = load i8, ptr %ptr.phi, align 1
  %add = add i8 %0, 1
  store i8 %add, ptr %ptr.phi, align 1
  %ptr.phi.next = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %ptr.phi, i64 1
  %cmp.i.not = icmp eq ptr %ptr.phi.next, %start
  %cmp = icmp ult i64 %index, %N
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %end, !llvm.loop !0

end:                                              ; preds = %for.body
  ret void
}

!0 = distinct !{!0, !1, !2, !3}
!1 = !{!"llvm.loop.interleave.count", i32 1}
!2 = !{!"llvm.loop.vectorize.width", i32 2}
!3 = !{!"llvm.loop.vectorize.scalable.enable", i1 true}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-linux-gnu"

%pair = type { ptr, ptr }

define void @test_pr55375_interleave_opaque_ptr(ptr %start, ptr %end) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi ptr [ %start, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %iv.1 = getelementptr inbounds %pair, ptr %iv, i64 0, i32 1
  store ptr %iv, ptr %iv.1, align 8
  store ptr null, ptr %iv, align 8
  %iv.next = getelementptr inbounds %pair, ptr %iv, i64 1
  %ec = icmp eq ptr %iv.next, %end
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

define void @store_pointer_induction(ptr %start, ptr %end) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi ptr [ %start, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  store ptr %iv, ptr %iv, align 4
  %iv.next = getelementptr inbounds ptr, ptr %iv, i32 1
  %exitcond = icmp eq ptr %iv.next, %end
  br i1 %exitcond, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}
